flowReports.notes: Direct API testing verified health endpoint returns ready status and auth-gating rejects requests without/invalid auth (401)
10 Playwright tests passed covering shell prompt submission, task handle stability, event streaming, auth scoping, reload reattachment, and duplicate submission prevention
Process kill/restart validated supervisor recovery and task state persistence across restarts

VAL-RUNTIME-002.evidence: 401 returned for missing/invalid auth; signed-out shell shows auth entry (not task runner)

VAL-RUNTIME-003.evidence: task submission returns stable handle with visible task ID in status display

VAL-RUNTIME-004.evidence: Task status display correlates to submitted handle (task ID visible and consistent)

VAL-RUNTIME-005.evidence: Event log visible with event items displayed; events stream accessible

VAL-RUNTIME-006.evidence: Each authenticated session sees only its own tasks; auth-gating enforced

VAL-RUNTIME-007.evidence: Completed task shows result in shell; provider-backed response received (stub provider in test, real provider configured via gateway for production)
VAL-RUNTIME-007.note: Tested with stub provider; real provider (Bedrock/Z.AI) available via gateway when AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK or ZAI_API_KEY configured

VAL-RUNTIME-008.evidence: Provider implementations return structured errors without crashing; runtime handles failed/blocked outcomes gracefully

VAL-RUNTIME-009.evidence: Supervisor recovery visible: kill sandbox process → service unavailable → restart → supervisor started (5s interval) → runtime recovered 3 tasks to failed state → health returned to ready
VAL-RUNTIME-009.limitations: Full Firecracker/KVM SSH testing requires Linux/Node B; local macOS testing validated core recovery logic

VAL-RUNTIME-010.evidence: SQLite persistence enables recovery; unit tests TestTaskRecoveryAcrossRestart and TestInterruptedRunningTasksRecoveredOnStart pass; runtime correctly recovered running tasks on restart

VAL-CROSS-109.evidence: Registration → shell → prompt submission → stable handle → status/event updates → final UI result; first useful runtime action completes end-to-end

VAL-CROSS-111.evidence: Page reload during in-flight task does not duplicate submission; submission count remains 1 after reload; reattachment works correctly

VAL-CROSS-121.evidence: Reload during in-flight work reattaches to same task ID without resubmission; task ID before and after reload matches; only 1 POST /api/agent/task request made

frictions.description: Provider credentials (AWS_BEARER_TOKEN_BEDROCK, ZAI_API_KEY) not available in test environment - tests run with stub provider
frictions.resolution: Stub provider validates runtime flow; real provider validated through gateway health and implementation review; production deployment uses real credentials via gateway

frictions.description: Full SSH/Firecracker testing requires Linux/Node B environment
frictions.resolution: Local macOS testing validated supervisor recovery logic through process kill/restart; full VM-based testing deferred to Node B deployment
